arts.ttl: Riccardo Calafiori Compares Serie A & Premier League
arts.sum: Riccardo Calafiori left Serie A in the summer to link up with Arsenal despite Thiago Motta's desperate attempt to lure him to Juventus. The Gunners paid Bologna £42 million to sign the versatile defender, who enjoyed a standout 2023/24 season at Renato Dall'Ara. The post Riccardo Calafiori Compares Serie A & Premier League appeared first on The Cult of Calcio.

arts.ttl: Arsenal predicted XI vs Leicester as Mikel Arteta weighs up key David Raya decision
arts.sum: Arsenal return to Premier League action on Saturday afternoon when Leicester visit the Emirates but the consequences from last weekend's draw at Manchester City continue to be felt

arts.ttl: Arteta ban warning, Guardiola scared as Arsenal vs Man City war of words erupts
arts.sum: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta rolled his eyes when there were more questions about his tactics and the use of so-called 'dark arts' during his press conference ahead of the game with Leicester City this weekend. Though Manchester City and Arsenal have been the best two teams in the Premier League for the last three years, it hasn't felt like a proper rivalry for two reasons.

arts.ttl: Arsenal guaranteed to be without six players for Leicester as David Raya decision 'confirmed'
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta knows his Arsenal side will need to be on top of their game against Leicester if they are to come away from Emirates Stadium with three points on Saturday afternoon. So, looking at the official Arsenal website, the Gunners have 16 fit senior players available for selection for the game at Leicester.

arts.ttl: Arsenal news: Mikel Arteta makes Pep Guardiola admission as Man City row rumbles on
arts.sum: David Raya is a major doubt for Arsenal's Premier League game against Leicester City on Saturday afternoon with Mikel Arteta still fielding questions about last weekend's draw against Manchester City

arts.ttl: Why is Arsenal vs Leicester not on TV in UK? How fans can watch Premier League match
arts.sum: Arsenal and Leicester City will both be eyeing up three Premier League points when they meet at the Emirates Stadium, but UK football fans won't be able to watch the game live on TV

arts.ttl: Arsenal v Leicester: FPL team news + predicted line-ups
arts.sum: Team form, absentees and our predicted XIs The post Arsenal v Leicester: FPL team news + predicted line-ups appeared first on Best FPL Tips, Advice, Team News, Picks, and Statistics from Fantasy Football Scout.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta makes promise to Pep Guardiola as Arsenal and Man City rivalry explodes
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta says there is no chance his friendship with Pep Guardiola will be destroyed by increasingly heated on-pitch battles between Arsenal and Manchester City

arts.ttl: Latest Arsenal injury news as six miss Leicester amid David Raya hint and Ben White update
arts.sum: Trossard, having served his one-match ban, will be back available to face Leicester and Arteta has now offered updates on both Raya and White along with Jurrien Timber. "Jurrien [Timber] was able to take part in training today, that’s good news, so he’ll be available in the squad.

arts.ttl: Ethan Nwaneri decision, Raya risk and Sterling chance - Arsenal line ups vs Leicester - Kaya Kaynak - football.london
arts.sum: Canton’s Arsenal XI: Raya; White, Saliba, Gabriel, Calafiori; Partey, Rice, Trossard; Saka, Havertz, Sterling Ben White returns at right-back and while Jurrien Timber may be in a better place after his rest against Bolton, I think continuing that trend ahead of the visit of PSG would be smart, especially as it gives another chance to Calafiori to play.
